categories.name: Foundations: Advanced Python
categories.description: Core Python patterns and engineering practices tuned for AI and data infrastructure.

topics.title: Decorators & Higher-Order Functions
topics.description: Wrapper patterns critical for logging, caching, rate limiting, and API middleware.
topics.why_important: Decorators are heavily utilized in Python backend frameworks (FastAPI) and machine learning libraries (PyTorch, LangChain) for logging, profiling, and routing without modifying the underlying function logic.

topics.title: Generators & Lazy Evaluation
topics.description: yield, the iterator protocol, and building memory-efficient data pipelines for ML.
topics.why_important: Crucial for handling massive datasets in ML engineering. They allow you to stream data through memory (like gigabytes of text for LLMs) without OOM (Out of Memory) crashes.

topics.title: Context Managers & Resources
topics.description: Handling DB connections, file streams, and API clients safely with async/sync contexts.
topics.why_important: Ensures that scarce resources like database connections, GPUs, or file handles are rigorously released even when critical errors or exceptions occur.

topics.title: Type Hints & Pydantic Validation
topics.description: Strict typing, generics, and Pydantic models for structured outputs and safe pipelines.
topics.why_important: LLMs output unstructured text. Pydantic is the industry standard for forcing LLM outputs into strictly validated JSON schemas, catching hallucinations instantly.

topics.title: Error Handling & Defensive Coding
topics.description: Custom exception hierarchies, global handlers, and graceful degradation.
topics.why_important: AI pipelines fail randomly due to API limits, network timeouts, and probabilistic model behaviors. Defensive coding stops a $10 inference run from crashing halfway.

topics.title: Structures: Hash Tables & Trees
topics.description: Dicts, sets, binary trees, heaps, and trie structures — complexity and ML use cases.
topics.why_important: The backbone of all ML. Vector databases are just advanced trees/hash structures. Knowing the time complexity prevents building systems that choke on 1M+ embeddings.

topics.title: Algorithms & Big-O Analysis
topics.description: Time/space complexity, sorting internals, BFS/DFS, and dynamic programming basics.
topics.why_important: Allows you to mathematically prove your retrieval function will scale linearly O(N) or logarithmically O(log N) as the enterprise data scales.

topics.title: Async Programming with asyncio
topics.description: Coroutines, event loops, tasks, semaphores, and concurrent AI integrations.
topics.why_important: LLM API calls are heavily I/O bound. If you use sync requests, your app blocks while waiting 5 seconds for GPT-4 to respond. Asyncio achieves massive concurrency.

topics.title: Testing, Mocking & CI/CD Pipelines
topics.description: pytest, fixtures, mocking external APIs, and practical SOLID principles in ML codebases.
topics.why_important: Testing non-deterministic AI behavior is insanely difficult. Strong testing prevents prompt regressions from breaking production systems.

categories.name: Software Architecture & System Design
categories.description: The structural blueprint for directing AI agents to build scalable, robust systems.

topics.title: Clean Architecture & SOLID
topics.description: Dependency injection, interfaces, and decoupling business logic from standard frameworks.
topics.why_important: When an AI writes code, tight coupling is extremely dangerous. Forcing the AI to use interfaces ensures it doesn't break 10 files when attempting to modify 1.

topics.title: Data Modeling: SQL, NoSQL, Vector
topics.description: Choosing the exact database paradigm for the specific system requirement.
topics.why_important: AI can write perfect SQL syntactically, but it is historically bad at high-level schema design. You must architect the schemas.

topics.title: Event-Driven Systems & Queues
topics.description: Pub/Sub architectures, message brokers, and asynchronous worker nodes.
topics.why_important: LLMs take 10-30 seconds to generate a response. Native HTTP requests timeout. You must implement robust asynchronous worker queues to handle tasks without blocking the server.

topics.title: API Design Contracts & Streaming
topics.description: Designing endpoints, REST, gRPC, and handling streaming protocols for LLMs.
topics.why_important: The UI needs to feel instantaneous. Streaming tokens back to the client via Server-Sent Events requires careful protocol design beyond basic REST endpoints.

topics.title: State Management & Agent Memory
topics.description: Persisting conversational state, checkpoints, and managing ephemeral cache.
topics.why_important: Agents must freeze and hydrate their state. If the server crashes mid-task, how do you restore the agent exactly where it left off? State machines map this.

categories.name: ML Fundamentals
categories.description: Classical machine learning concepts anchoring deep learning intuition.

topics.title: Linear & Logistic Regression: Math to Code
topics.description: Cost functions, gradient descent, normal equation, sigmoids, and building optimization intuition.
topics.why_important: Provides the fundamental intuition for how models fundamentally "learn" by navigating high-dimensional error spaces.

topics.title: Bias, Variance & Overfitting
topics.description: Learning curves, regularization, the bias-variance decomposition, and diagnostics.
topics.why_important: The core diagnostic tool when your model fails. It tells you exactly whether you need more data, a bigger model, or more regularization.

topics.title: Eval Metrics: F1, ROC, & Embeddings
topics.description: Precision, recall, confusion matrices, ROC-AUC, and how classical metrics map to vector retrieval.
topics.why_important: Accuracy is a lie in imbalanced datasets. These metrics are the foundation for evaluating RAG retrieval systems (Precision@K, Recall).

topics.title: Tree Ensembles vs Neural Networks
topics.description: XGBoost, Random Forests, bagging vs boosting, and when trees absolutely beat deep learning.
topics.why_important: For tabular business data, Random Forest and XGBoost still routinely outperform complex Deep Learning models with significantly less computation.

categories.name: Deep Learning Internals
categories.description: Neural network mechanics focused on what powers modern LLMs.

topics.title: Activations & The Forward Pass
topics.description: MLPs, activations (ReLU, GELU), universal approximation, and exact forward pass mechanics.
topics.why_important: The foundational building block of neural networks. Understanding matrix multiplication here demystifies the black box of LLMs.

topics.title: Backprop & Gradient Flow
topics.description: Chain rule, computational graphs, and diagnosing exploding/vanishing gradients.
topics.why_important: The algorithm that powers the AI revolution. Understanding gradient flow helps diagnose why a model's loss is NaN or why learning stagnates.

topics.title: Loss & Optimizers: AdamW and Learning Rates
topics.description: Cross-entropy, MSE, Adam, weight decay, and LR schedules (warmups, cosine).
topics.why_important: Determines HOW the model learns. AdamW is the default engine tuning billions of parameters in modern language models.

topics.title: The Road to Transformers: CNNs & RNNs
topics.description: Convolutions, hidden states, LSTMs, and the vanishing gradient bottleneck that forced attention.
topics.why_important: To truly appreciate the self-attention mechanism, you must understand the architectural bottlenecks of RNNs (sequential processing) that preceded it.

topics.title: PyTorch: Tensors & Training Loops
topics.description: Autograd, nn.Module, DataLoaders, and mapping math to valid tensor operations.
topics.why_important: The undisputed king of AI research and engineering. Understanding autograd and tensors translates directly to manipulating LLM layers.

categories.name: NLP to LLMs
categories.description: The evolution of the NLP pipeline leading directly into Transformers.

topics.title: Tokenization: BPE & Subwords
topics.description: BPE, WordPiece, tiktoken, Out-Of-Vocabulary handling, and why models can't spell "strawberry".
topics.why_important: LLMs don't read words, they read tokens. Tokenization explains why LLMs struggle with rhyming, math, and specific language nuances.

topics.title: Embeddings: Word2Vec to BERT
topics.description: The distributional hypothesis, static embeddings, and the breakthrough of context-aware vectors.
topics.why_important: Embeddings convert raw text into spatial geometry. This is the cornerstone of all retrieval augmented generation (RAG) and semantic search.

topics.title: Self-Attention & Multi-Head Attention
topics.description: Query-key-value math, scaled dot-product, masking, and visual intuition for attention.
topics.why_important: The breakthrough that allowed models to weigh the importance of every word against every other word simultaneously, avoiding sequential memory bottlenecks.

topics.title: Transformer Architecture Deep Dive
topics.description: Positional encoding, layer norm, residual connections, and building from scratch.
topics.why_important: The ubiquitous architecture scaling laws rely upon. Understanding the encoder/decoder blocks is mandatory for any serious GenAI engineer.

topics.title: BERT vs GPT Paradigm Shift
topics.description: MLM vs Autoregressive, Encoders vs Decoders, and why decoder-only architectures scaled.
topics.why_important: Explains the divergence in AI. BERT evolved into bidirectional search/encoders, while GPT evolved into autoregressive generative decoders.

topics.title: Fine-Tuning & LoRA
topics.description: Full FT vs PEFT, LoRA rank mechanics, QLoRA, and practical workflows for specific domains.
topics.why_important: Training from scratch costs millions. Fine-tuning with LoRA allows injecting specialized domain knowledge into massive models on a single GPU.

categories.name: GenAI & LLM Production
categories.description: The absolute core: generative AI patterns, pipelines, and agents.

topics.title: GenAI Architectures & Scaling
topics.description: Chinchilla scaling laws, MoE (Mixture of Experts) basics, and data vs parameter tradeoffs.
topics.why_important: Provides the blueprint for how a 70 Billion parameter matrix math engine becomes a chat assistant hosted on secure infrastructure.

topics.title: Context Windows & Economics
topics.description: Lost-in-the-middle phenomena, RoPE positioning limits, API tier economies, and active context management.
topics.why_important: You pay by the token. Mismanaging the context window rapidly leads to massive cloud bills and models "forgetting" instructions stuck in the middle.

topics.title: Temperature, Top-P & Decoding
topics.description: Greedy vs stochastic decoding, adjusting randomness per task, and logit bias overrides.
topics.why_important: Temperature and Top-P dictate the "creativity" or "hallucination" rate of outputs. Controlling decoding prevents deterministic agents from entering infinite loops.

topics.title: Prompt Foundations & Anatomy
topics.description: System prompts, zero-shot/few-shot design, and establishing robust output instructions.
topics.why_important: The primary interface for steering LLMs. Effective system prompts heavily mitigate downstream hallucinations and erratic behavior.

topics.title: Structured Output & JSON Mode
topics.description: Constraining generation, extracting strict JSON, tools vs JSON mode, and handling parse failures.
topics.why_important: Without strict JSON enforcement, it is impossible to programmatically chain LLM outputs into deterministic application workflows or databases.

topics.title: Advanced Prompt Engineering
topics.description: Chain-of-Thought, Tree-of-Thought, self-consistency, and iterative refinement patterns.
topics.why_important: Chain of Thought significantly boosts reasoning by mimicking the scratchpad intuition of human thought processes.

topics.title: RAG Phase 1: Chunking & Indexing
topics.description: Semantic chunking, metadata attachment, handling tables/images, and vector DB insertion.
topics.why_important: Garbage in, garbage out. If a PDF is chunked haphazardly, the optimal answer gets split in half and the semantic search fails completely.

topics.title: RAG Phase 2: Retrieval & Reranking
topics.description: Dense/Sparse hybrid search, BM25, Reciprocal Rank Fusion, Cross-Encoder reranking, and query expansion.
topics.why_important: Vector similarity relies on generic embeddings. Rerankers act as a second-pass cross-checker to drastically spike accuracy before hitting the LLM.

topics.title: RAG Phase 3: Generation & Evaluation
topics.description: Context injection, grounding prompts, source attribution workflows, and RAGAS framework metrics.
topics.why_important: If you can't measure your RAG system, you can't improve it. RAGAS provides quantitative scores (faithfulness, relevancy) to prove system reliability.

topics.title: Agent Architectures & Tool Calling
topics.description: ReAct, Tool/Function Calling loops, state management, and memory buffer types.
topics.why_important: Allows an LLM to step outside a sandbox. Tool calling transforms a passive chat interface into an autonomous workflow execution framework.

topics.title: Multi-Agent Orchestration
topics.description: Supervisor nodes, graph-based routing, debate patterns, and avoiding infinite agent loops.
topics.why_important: Single agents get confused on massive tasks. Multi-agent routing creates networks of specialized, high-performing experts communicating directly.

topics.title: Benchmarking & LLM-as-a-Judge
topics.description: Standard benchmarks, pairwise evals, establishing golden datasets, and automating judge prompts.
topics.why_important: Manual vibe checks don't scale. Automated LLM-as-a-judge pipelines are critical to ensure that a new prompt doesn't secretly break older features.

topics.title: Output Guardrails & Safety
topics.description: Hallucination catchers, programmatic validation, and semantic fallbacks for toxic/bad output.
topics.why_important: Customer-facing AI that says inappropriate things creates massive brand risk. Guardrails act as an absolute hard boundary against bad outputs.

topics.title: Security: Prompt Injection & Leakage
topics.description: Direct/indirect prompt injection mechanisms, system prompt leakage, and mitigation strategies.
topics.why_important: LLMs execute commands via natural language, making them inherently vulnerable to malicious user strings (e.g., 'Delete the database').

categories.name: Integration & System Design
categories.description: Bridging modules: How pieces wire together to form resilient, production-ready AI systems.

topics.title: Anatomy of an E2E GenAI App
topics.description: Tying APIs + RAG + Agents + Frontends + Monitoring into one coherent blueprint.
topics.why_important: Connects isolated components into a full resilient backend spanning UI, orchestrator, vector DB, and the LLM inference engine.

topics.title: Debugging LLM Applications
topics.description: Tracing root causes for hallucinations: was it the prompt, retrieval, chunking, or model choice?
topics.why_important: Because AI is non-deterministic, figuring out exactly *which* tool in a 6-step agent chain failed requires extremely methodical debugging protocols.

topics.title: Resiliency & Error Handling in GenAI
topics.description: Semantic retries, circuit breakers, fallback models, and graceful degradation when APIs drop.
topics.why_important: APIs go down and rate limits hit. Fallbacks ensure that if GPT-4 fails, the system automatically routes to Claude or drops gracefully.

topics.title: LLM Caching & Cost Optimization
topics.description: Semantic vs exact caching, prompt caching systems, and slicing latency/cost at scale.
topics.why_important: LLMs are incredibly slow and expensive. Caching identical requests drastically cuts down cloud provider bills and drops latency to milliseconds.

topics.title: API Design for AI Systems
topics.description: Async streaming (SSE), WebSockets, background tasks, routing, and Docker fundamentals.
topics.why_important: If you host an open-source model (like Llama 3), you need an inference engine that scales to thousands of concurrent users.

topics.title: Monitoring & Observability
topics.description: LangSmith basics, metrics logging, token tracking, and detecting concept drift in production.
topics.why_important: Data drift is real. Without observability, your model could slowly degenerate in quality over months while users quietly abandon the platform.

topics.title: Vector Databases: HNSW & Scaling
topics.description: How vector DBs actually work under the hood, indexing trade-offs, and comparing managed providers.
topics.why_important: When data hits 10+ million documents, standard Postgres chokes on vector similarities lacking specifically tuned indexes.

categories.name: Tool Proficiency
categories.description: Mastering the frameworks and workflows that multiply engineering output.

topics.title: AI Assistants: Workflows & Power Patterns
topics.description: Cursor/Antigravity power patterns, iterating context window effectively, and extreme rapid prototyping.
topics.why_important: AI natively disrupts engineering. Leveraging Cursor/Copilot effectively accelerates your velocity 10x, moving you from coder to architect.

topics.title: LangChain & LCEL
topics.description: Chains, templates, parsing, avoiding abstractions overhead, and production integration.
topics.why_important: The dominant framework in the industry. It provides standardized abstractions to rapidly prototype LLMs, prompts, and memory.

topics.title: LangGraph: Stateful Workflows
topics.description: Building cyclic graphs, state persistence, conditional edges, and orchestrating complex workflows.
topics.why_important: Stateful agent flows. Standard Langchain chains are linear DAGs; Langgraph allows cycles (loops) which are mandatory for autonomous agents.

topics.title: Building Servers with FastAPI
topics.description: Spinning up clean APIs natively integrated with Pydantic for high-performance ML serving.
topics.why_important: The definitive Python backend framework. Its native integration with async and Pydantic makes it the ideal host for any AI endpoint.

topics.title: Cloud Vendor Ecosystems
topics.description: Navigating Azure AI, AWS Bedrock, GCP Vertex: APIs, permissions, and deployment topologies.
topics.why_important: Modern startups orchestrate across managed clouds (AWS Bedrock, Azure OpenAI) to avoid DevOps overhead and secure enterprise deployments.
